explanation: Highly expressed in plasma cells, involved in antibody secretion.
Essential for IgA and IgM polymerization, specific to plasma cells.
Also known as CD138, a canonical plasma cell surface marker.
Expressed on plasma cells and used to distinguish them from other B cells.
Highly expressed on plasma cells, used as a diagnostic marker.
A pan-epithelial marker highly expressed in epithelial and carcinoma cells.
Also known as CD133, a marker of cancer stem cells in colorectal cancer.
A mucin gene upregulated in colorectal adenocarcinoma epithelial cells.
Overexpressed in various cancers, including colorectal cancer epithelial cells.
Frequently upregulated in colorectal cancer and associated with tumor progression.
A cell surface marker enriched in colorectal cancer stem and epithelial cells.
Carboxypeptidase A3 is a classic mast cell-specific protease.
Hematopoietic prostaglandin D synthase is highly expressed in mast cells.
Also known as ST2, a receptor expressed on mast cells.
Transcription factor essential for mast cell development.
Leukotriene C4 synthase is involved in mast cell mediator synthesis.
Regulator of G-protein signaling enriched in mast cells.
Key receptor for macrophage colony-stimulating factor, highly expressed in macrophages.
Co-receptor for LPS, classic monocyte/macrophage marker.
Complement component highly expressed in tissue macrophages.
Also known as IBA1, a microglia/macrophage marker.
Fc gamma receptor III, expressed in monocytes/macrophages.
Amplifies inflammatory responses in myeloid cells, including macrophages.
Classical marker of fibroblasts, especially in colorectal cancer stroma.
Encodes type III collagen, highly expressed in fibroblasts and extracellular matrix-producing cells.
Frequently marks fibroblasts in colorectal and other tissues.
Another key fibroblast marker, often co-expressed with PDGFRA in stromal cells.
Extracellular matrix protein, enriched in fibroblasts.
Wnt signaling modulator, expressed in fibroblasts within tumor stroma.
Encodes the CD8 alpha chain, a definitive marker of CD8+ cytotoxic T cells.
Granzyme A is a cytotoxic molecule expressed by activated CD8+ T cells.
A pan-T cell marker, present on all T cells including CD8+ subsets.
Part of the T cell receptor-CD3 complex, essential for T cell activation.
An inhibitory receptor commonly expressed on activated and exhausted CD8+ T cells.
A co-stimulatory molecule upregulated on activated T cells, including CD8+ subsets.
